word_list.pronunciation: bˈɜːn ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ tʊ ɐ kɹˈɪsp ɔːɹ sˈɪndɚ

senses.definition: (of food) to be burned completely, due to being cooked too much or for too long

examples.x: The fire was so intense that it burned the entire forest to a crisp, leaving behind a barren landscape.

senses.definition: to eat an excessive amount of food

examples.x: Despite her petite figure, Mary eats like a horse, often finishing multiple plates of food during meals.
After a long day of physical labor, John comes home and eats like a pig, devouring everything in sight.
Despite being on a diet, Tom couldn't resist the buffet and ate like a pig, regretting it afterward.

senses.definition: to eat so much of food available in someone's house so that there is little or none left

examples.x: Whenever my teenage son's friends come over, they eat me out of house and home, finishing all the snacks and raiding the refrigerator.
Whenever my teenage son's friends come over, they eat me out of house and home, finishing all the snacks and raiding the refrigerator.
During the holiday season, my extended family visits, and with their appetites, they eat us out of house and home, leaving the pantry empty.
When my nephews visit, they eat like there's no tomorrow, and within a few days, they've eaten us out of house and home.

senses.definition: to eat more food than one's body needs or can handle

examples.x: After a long day at work, I just want to go home, put my feet up, and feed my face with a delicious meal.
Despite being on a diet, John couldn't resist the temptation and gave in, feeding his face with a big slice of chocolate cake.
At the buffet, John made a pig of himself, piling his plate high with food and going back for multiple servings.
Despite having a small appetite, Mary made a pig of herself at the all-you-can-eat restaurant, determined to get her money's worth.
I can't wait to get to grandma's and put the old feedbag on.
It's lunchtime, so let's put the old feedbag on and grab a bite to eat.
After a long day of work, I can't wait to put the feedbag on and enjoy a delicious dinner.
The family gathered around the table, ready to put the feedbag on and enjoy the home-cooked meal.
We arrived at the restaurant hungry and ready to put the feedbag on, ordering a variety of dishes to satisfy our appetites.

senses.definition: to eat or drink to the point where one cannot keep eating or drinking anymore

examples.x: Sir, I believe you just drank your fill.
After a long day of hiking, we sat by the campfire and ate our fill of roasted marshmallows.
At the all-you-can-eat buffet, she made sure to eat her fill of the delicious seafood dishes.
The farmer invited us to pick as many apples as we wanted, and we ate to our fill right there in the orchard.

senses.definition: to have a very little desire to eat

examples.x: She always claims to be on a diet, but in reality, she eats like a bird and hardly consumes anything substantial.
Whenever we go out to eat, he orders a small salad and eats like a bird, while the rest of us enjoy hearty meals.
Even though the party had a buffet with a variety of dishes, he ate like a bird, trying a little bit of everything but not taking much.
I was surprised when he finished the large pizza all by himself; I thought he would eat like a bird, but he has a big appetite.

senses.definition: (of odors or smells) to travel through the air and being detected by someone's sense of smell

examples.x: The stench of the garbage reached my nostrils as soon as I entered the room.
The pungent smell of burning rubber reached my nostrils, indicating that something was wrong with my car.
As I walked by the flower shop, the delightful fragrance of roses reached my nostrils, brightening my day.
The foul odor of decay reached our nostrils as we approached the abandoned building, making us hesitate to enter.

senses.definition: to quickly make a meal for oneself, often due to a time constraint

examples.x: Nothing's better than grabbing a bite to eat with friends.
Let's grab a bite to eat before the movie; we don't have much time.
He suggested grabbing a bite at the food truck for a quick lunch during the break.
I'm too tired to cook tonight; let's just grab a bite from the nearby takeout place.

senses.definition: the ability to eat a wide variety of foods without experiencing digestive problems

examples.x: Even though he ate street food from various vendors during his travels, he never got sick – he has a cast-iron stomach.
She can handle the spiciest dishes without any discomfort; it's like she has a cast-iron stomach.
I wish I had a cast-iron stomach like her; I'm so sensitive to certain foods.
His cast-iron stomach allows him to enjoy exotic and sometimes unusual foods without worry.
After years of working in the food industry, she developed a cast-iron stomach and could eat just about anything without feeling queasy.

senses.definition: a meal that is complete and satisfying

examples.x: After a long day of hiking, I was famished and enjoyed a square meal of grilled chicken, rice, and vegetables.
The restaurant's Sunday brunch offers a square meal with a variety of breakfast and lunch options.
In my grandmother's house, you could always count on a square meal, complete with a meat dish, vegetables, and homemade bread.
When you're out camping, a square meal by the campfire can be a comforting experience.
The school cafeteria has been working to provide students with healthier and more nutritious square meals.

senses.definition: to develop a liking for a particular type of cuisine or dish after trying it

examples.x: After visiting Thailand, I got a taste for spicy dishes, and now I can't get enough of Thai cuisine.
At first, I wasn't a fan of sushi, but after trying it a few times, I've really gotten a taste for it.
She never liked coffee before, but after her trip to Italy, she got a taste for espresso and now enjoys it every morning.
It took a while, but he finally got a taste for Indian curries and now craves them regularly.

senses.definition: used to refer to a cup of coffee

examples.x: I can't even function in the morning until I've had my first cup of joe.
I need a strong cup of joe to wake me up in the morning.
After a long night of work, a hot cup of joe is just what I crave.
The diner down the street serves the best cup of joe in town.
He offered her a cup of joe to keep warm during the cold winter morning.

word_list.pronunciation: sˈɪt wˈɛl ɹˈaɪt wɪð ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to be compatible with someone or something's stomach

examples.x: Eating a big meal right before bed doesn't sit right with me - I always end up feeling bloated and uncomfortable.
I always feel good after eating a salad - it sits well with my stomach.
Spicy food doesn't sit well with me.
